Verified Petition For Determination Of Intestate Succession The Estate Of Stepmother Rosalie Ellen Messick By Petitioner Joseph Mcfadden, Stepchild Of
Off Calendar Mar-02-2020 Continued To May-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Sauers for Respondent, Damien Arthur.
Aimee Morris for Petitioner, Joseph McFadden.

Ms. Morris stated that her client in school back east, that the sent her reply to the Probate Examiner by overnight mail, that she included 4 proof of service, and sent copies of documents for related case #, and the release and waiver of distributee by Clare Brown. The Court inquired if the Trustee is objecting. Mr. Sauers stated his client takes no position, that Luke is in poor shape, and Clare is the appointed conservator (or guardian as they are the same,) in Massachusetts. Ms. Morris stated the person who drafted the obituary and other family members want to come forward in support, that Joseph's father is still alive, that she is working on reaching him and and records such as school records showing Rosalie was his mother. The Court noted that notice should be sent to Luke as well, that an ex parte petition to consolidate and new filings should be filed in the 2012 case #. The Court inquired about notice to the charities. Ms. Morris stated there are 2 buckets of money kept separately for the 17 dividuals and the 20+ charities, that the 3rd bucket of money was for the trustor's son who died last year and his share was divided 50/50 to the individuals and the charities, and her client is not entitled to the money in the charities bucket.

The verified petition for determination of intestate succession to the estate of stepmother Rosalie Ellen Messick is continued to May 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by April 2, 2020 and courtesy copies delivered to Dept 202 by April 27, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.
